 31→import java.nio.file.FileSystems
 32→import java.nio.file.Path
 33→
 34→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 35→
 36→public class KtLintRuleEngine(
 37→    /**
 38→     * The set of [RuleProvider]s to be invoked by the [KtLintRuleEngine]. A [RuleProvider] is able to create a new instance of a [Rule] so
 39→     * that it can keep internal state and be called thread-safe manner
 40→     */
 41→    public val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
 42→    /**
 43→     * The default values for `.editorconfig` properties which are not set explicitly in any '.editorconfig' file located on the path of the
 44→     * file which is processed with the [KtLintRuleEngine]. If a property is set in [editorConfigDefaults] this takes precedence above the
 45→     * default values defined in the KtLint project.
 46→     */
 47→    public val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 48→    /**
 49→     * Override values for `.editorconfig` properties. If a property is set in [editorConfigOverride] it takes precedence above the same
 50→     * property being set in any other way.
 51→     */
 52→    public val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 53→    /**
 54→     * Temporary flag to indicate that kotlin embeddable compiler is to be executed with (default) or without the extension point
 55→     * 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler'. This extension point is not (yet) supported in the preview of Kotlin 1.9. Some
 56→     * rules might no longer work and throw exceptions at runtime.
 57→     * It is unclear whether the extension point will be supported. Disabling this flag on the Kotlin 1.8 compiler has the same effect. As
 58→     * of that it can be used to assess the impact, and to fix rules before release of the Ktlint version which will be based on Kotlin 1.9.
 59→     */
 60→    public val enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint: Boolean = true,
 61→    /**
 62→     * **For internal use only**: indicates that linting was invoked from KtLint CLI tool. It enables some internals workarounds for Kotlin
 63→     * Compiler initialization. This property is likely to be removed in any of next versions without further notice.
 64→     */
 65→    public val isInvokedFromCli: Boolean = false,
 66→    /**
 67→     * The [FileSystem] to be used. This property is primarily intended to be used in unit tests. By specifying an alternative [FileSystem]
 68→     * the unit test gains control on whether the [EditorConfigLoader] should or should not read specific ".editorconfig" files. For
 69→     * example, it is considered unwanted that a unit test is influenced by the ".editorconfig" file of the project in which the unit test
 70→     * is included.
 71→     */
 72→    public val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 73→) {
 74→    init {
 75→        require(ruleProviders.any()) {
 76→            "A non-empty set of 'ruleProviders' need to be provided"
 77→        }
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    internal val editorConfigLoaderEc4j = EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
 81→
 82→    internal val editorConfigLoader =
 83→        EditorConfigLoader(
 84→            fileSystem,
 85→            editorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 86→            editorConfigDefaults,
 87→            editorConfigOverride,
 88→        )
 89→
 90→    /**
 91→     * Check the [code] for lint errors. If [code] is path as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on the path to file are taken
 92→     * into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
 93→     *
 94→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
 95→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
 96→     */
 97→    public fun lint(
 98→        code: Code,
 99→        callback: (LintError) -> Unit = { },
100→    ) {
101→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
102→
103→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
104→        val errors = mutableListOf<LintError>()
105→
106→        VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
107→            .visitor()
108→            .invoke { rule ->
109→                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, false) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
110→                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
111→                    errors.add(LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected))
112→                }
113→            }
114→
115→        errors
116→            .sortedWith { l, r -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
117→            .forEach { e -> callback(e) }
118→
119→        LOGGER.debug { "Finished with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
120→    }
121→
122→    /**
123→     * Fix style violations in [code] for lint errors when possible. If [code] is passed as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on
124→     * the path are taken into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
125→     *
126→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
127→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
128→     */
129→    public fun format(
130→        code: Code,
131→        callback: (LintError, Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _ -> },
132→    ): String {
133→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
134→
135→        val hasUTF8BOM = code.content.startsWith(UTF8_BOM)
136→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
137→
138→        var tripped = false
139→        var mutated = false
140→        val errors = mutableSetOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
141→        val visitorProvider = VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
142→        visitorProvider
143→            .visitor()
144→            .invoke { rule ->
145→                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
146→                    tripped = true
147→                    if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
148→                        mutated = true
149→                        /**
150→                         * Rebuild the suppression locator after each change in the AST as the offsets of the
151→                         * suppression hints might have changed.
152→                         */
153→                        ruleExecutionContext.rebuildSuppressionLocator()
154→                    }
155→                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
156→                    errors.add(
157→                        Pair(
158→                            LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
159→                            // It is assumed that a rule that emits that an error can be autocorrected, also
160→                            // does correct the error.
161→                            canBeAutoCorrected,
162→                        ),
163→                    )
164→                    // In trace mode report the violation immediately. The order in which violations are actually found might be different
165→                    // from the order in which they are reported. For debugging purposes it cn be helpful to know the exact order in which
166→                    // violations are being solved.
167→                    LOGGER.trace {
168→                        "Format violation: ${code.fileNameOrStdin()}:$line:$col: $errorMessage (${rule.ruleId})" +
169→                            canBeAutoCorrected.ifFalse { " [cannot be autocorrected]" }
170→                    }
171→                }
172→            }
173→        if (tripped) {
174→            visitorProvider
175→                .visitor()
176→                .invoke { rule ->
177→                    ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(
178→                        rule,
179→                        false,
180→                    ) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
181→                        val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
182→                        errors.add(
183→                            Pair(
184→                                LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
185→                                // It is assumed that a rule only corrects an error after it has emitted an
186→                                // error and indicating that it actually can be autocorrected.
187→                                false,
188→                            ),
189→                        )
190→                    }
191→                }
192→        }
193→
194→        errors
195→            .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
196→            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
197→
198→        if (!mutated) {
199→            return code.content
200→        }
201→
202→        val formattedCode =
203→            ruleExecutionContext
204→                .rootNode
205→                .text
206→                .replace("\n", ruleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(code.content))
207→        return if (hasUTF8BOM) {
208→            UTF8_BOM + formattedCode
209→        } else {
210→            formattedCode
211→        }.also {
212→            LOGGER.debug("Finished with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'")
213→        }
214→    }
215→
216→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
217→        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
218→        return when {
219→            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
220→            else -> "\n"
221→        }
222→    }
223→
224→    /**
225→     * Generates Kotlin `.editorconfig` file section content based on a path to a file or directory. Given that path, all '.editorconfig'
226→     * files on that path are taken into account to determine the values of properties which are already used.
227→     *
228→     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
229→     * ```properties
230→     * final-newline=true
231→     * indent-size=4
232→     * ```
233→     */
234→    public fun generateKotlinEditorConfigSection(filePath: Path): String {
235→        val codeStyle =
236→            editorConfigOverride
237→                .properties[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
238→                ?.parsed
239→                ?.safeAs<CodeStyleValue>()
240→                ?: CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
241→        val rules =
242→            ruleProviders
243→                .map { it.createNewRuleInstance() }
244→                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
245→                .toSet()
246→        return EditorConfigGenerator(fileSystem, editorConfigLoaderEc4j)
247→            .generateEditorconfig(
248→                rules,
249→                codeStyle,
250→                filePath,
251→            )
252→    }
253→
254→    /**
255→     * Reduce memory usage by cleaning internal caches.
256→     */
257→    public fun trimMemory() {
258→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.clear()
259→    }
260→
261→    /**
262→     * Get the list of files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting the given file or directory.
263→     * The API consumer can use this list to observe changes in '.editorconfig' files. Whenever such a change is
264→     * observed, the API consumer should call [reloadEditorConfigFile].
265→     * To avoid unnecessary access to the file system, it is best to call this method only once for the root of the
266→     * project which is to be [lint] or [format].
267→     */
268→    public fun editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path> = EditorConfigFinder(editorConfigLoaderEc4j).findEditorConfigs(path)
269→
270→    /**
271→     * Reloads an '.editorconfig' file given that it is currently loaded into the KtLint cache. This method is intended
272→     * to be called by the API consumer when it is aware of changes in the '.editorconfig' file that should be taken
273→     * into account with next calls to [lint] and/or [format]. See [editorConfigFilePaths] to get the list of
274→     * '.editorconfig' files which need to be observed.
275→     */
276→    public fun reloadEditorConfigFile(path: Path) {
277→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.reloadIfExists(
278→            Resource.Resources.ofPath(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8),
279→        )
280→    }
281→
282→    public fun transformToAst(code: Code): FileASTNode = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code).rootNode
283→
284→    public companion object {
285→        internal const val UTF8_BOM = "\uFEFF"
286→
287→        public const val STDIN_FILE: String = "<stdin>"
288→    }
289→}
290→

1→!!! important
  2→    Up and until Ktlint version `0.47`, experimental were located in a separate `experimental` rule set. As of Ktlint version `0.48`, each rule set can optionally define experimental rules.
  3→
  4→All experimental rules described below are part of the `standard` rule set of Ktlint. To enable all experimental rules (from all rule sets), set `editorconfig` property below:
  5→```ini
  6→[*.{kt,kts}]
  7→ktlint_experimental=enabled
  8→```
  9→Also see [enable/disable specific rules](../configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules).
 10→
 11→## Binary expression wrapping
 12→
 13→Wraps binary expression at the operator reference whenever the binary expression does not fit on the line. In case the binary expression is nested, the expression is evaluated from outside to inside. If the left and right hand sides of the binary expression, after wrapping, fit on a single line then the inner binary expressions will not be wrapped. If one or both inner binary expression still do not fit on a single after wrapping of the outer binary expression, then each of those inner binary expressions will be wrapped.
 14→
 15→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 16→
 17→    ```kotlin
 18→    fun foo() {
 19→        // Assume that the last allowed character is
 20→        // at the X character on the right                       X
 21→        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) ||
 22→            (leftHandSideLongExpression &&
 23→                rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
 24→            // do something
 25→        }
 26→    }
 27→    ```
 28→
 29→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 30→
 31→    ```kotlin
 32→    fun foo() {
 33→        // Assume that the last allowed character is
 34→        // at the X character on the right                       X
 35→        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) || (leftHandSideLongExpression && rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
 36→            // do something
 37→        }
 38→    }
 39→    ```
 40→
 41→Rule id: `binary-expression-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
 42→
 43→## Blank line before declarations
 44→
 45→Requires a blank line before any class or function declaration. No blank line is required between the class signature and the first declaration in the class. In a similar way, a blank line is required before any list of top level or class properties. No blank line is required before local properties or between consecutive properties.
 46→
 47→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 48→
 49→    ```kotlin
 50→    const val foo1 = "foo1"
 51→
 52→    class FooBar {
 53→        val foo2 = "foo2"
 54→        val foo3 = "foo3"
 55→
 56→        fun bar1() {
 57→           val foo4 = "foo4"
 58→           val foo5 = "foo5"
 59→        }
 60→
 61→        fun bar2() = "bar"
 62→
 63→        val foo6 = "foo3"
 64→        val foo7 = "foo4"
 65→
 66→        enum class Foo {}
 67→    }
 68→    ```
 69→
 70→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 71→
 72→    ```kotlin
 73→    const val foo1 = "foo1"
 74→    class FooBar {
 75→        val foo2 = "foo2"
 76→        val foo3 = "foo3"
 77→        fun bar1() {
 78→           val foo4 = "foo4"
 79→           val foo5 = "foo5"
 80→        }
 81→        fun bar2() = "bar"
 82→        val foo6 = "foo3"
 83→        val foo7 = "foo4"
 84→        enum class Foo {}
 85→    }
 86→    ```
 87→
 88→Rule id: `blank-line-before-declaration` (`standard` rule set)
 89→
 90→!!! Note
 91→This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 92→
 93→## Discouraged comment location
 94→
 95→Detect discouraged comment locations (no autocorrect).
 96→
 97→!!! note
 98→    Kotlin allows comments to be placed almost everywhere. As this can lead to code which is hard to read, most of them will never be used in practice. Ideally each rule takes comments at all possible locations into account. Sometimes this is really hard and not worth the effort. By explicitly forbidding such comment locations, the development of those rules becomes a bit easier. 
 99→
100→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
101→
102→    ```kotlin
103→    fun <T> /* some comment */ foo(t: T) = "some-result"
104→
105→    fun foo() {
106→        if (true)
107→            // some comment
108→            bar()
109→    }
110→    ```
111→
112→Rule id: `discouraged-comment-location` (`standard` rule set)
113→
114→## Disallow empty lines at start of class body
115→
116→Detect blank lines at start of a class body.
117→
118→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
119→
120→    ```kotlin
121→    class Foo {
122→        val foo = "foo"
123→    }
124→    ```
125→
126→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
127→
128→    ```kotlin
129→    class Foo {
130→
131→        val foo = "foo"
132→    }
133→    ```
134→
135→Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` (`standard` rule set)
136→
137→!!! Note
138→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
139→
140→## Disallow consecutive comments
141→
142→Consecutive comments are disallowed in following cases:
143→- Any mix of a consecutive kdoc, a block comment or an EOL comment unless separated by a blank line in between
144→- Consecutive KDocs (even when separated by a blank line)
145→- Consecutive block comments (even when separated by a blank line)
146→
147→Consecutive EOL comments are always allowed as they are often used instead of a block comment.
148→
149→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
150→
151→    ```kotlin
152→    // An EOL comment
153→    // may be followed by another EOL comment
154→    val foo = "foo"
155→
156→    // Different comment types (including KDoc) may be consecutive ..
157→
158→    /*
159→     * ... but do need to be separated by a blank line ...
160→     */
161→
162→    /**
163→      * ... but a KDoc can not be followed by an EOL or a block comment or another KDoc
164→      */
165→    fun bar() = "bar"
166→
167→    ```
168→
169→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
170→
171→    ```kotlin
172→    /*
173→     * Block comments can not be consecutive ...
174→     */
175→    /*
176→     * ... even not when separated by a new line.
177→     */
178→    val bar = "bar" 
179→
180→    /**
181→      * A KDoc can not be followed by a block comment or an EOL comment or another KDOC
182→      */
183→
184→    // ... even not when separated by a new line.
185→    ```
186→
187→Rule id: `no-consecutive-comments` (`standard` rule set)
188→
189→!!! Note
190→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
191→
192→## Function signature
193→
194→Rewrites the function signature to a single line when possible (e.g. when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property) or a multiline signature otherwise. In case of function with a body expression, the body expression is placed on the same line as the function signature when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property. Optionally the function signature can be forced to be written as a multiline signature in case the function has more than a specified number of parameters (`.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters`)
195→
196→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
197→
198→    ```kotlin
199→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
200→    // at the X character on the right           X
201→    fun foooooooo(
202→        a: Any,
203→        b: Any,
204→        c: Any
205→    ): String {
206→        // body
207→    }
208→
209→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
210→    // at the X character on the right           X
211→    fun bar(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
212→        // body
213→    }
214→
215→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
216→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
217→    // at the X character on the right           X
218→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
219→        .uppercase()
220→
221→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
222→    // or 'always'.
223→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
224→    // at the X character on the right           X
225→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
226→        "some-result"
227→            .uppercase()
228→    ```
229→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
230→
231→    ```kotlin
232→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
233→    // at the X character on the right           X
234→    fun foooooooo(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
235→        // body
236→    }
237→
238→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
239→    // at the X character on the right           X
240→    fun bar(
241→        a: Any,
242→        b: Any,
243→        c: Any
244→    ): String {
245→        // body
246→    }
247→
248→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
249→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
250→    // at the X character on the right           X
251→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
252→        "some-result"
253→            .uppercase()
254→
255→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
256→    // or 'always'.
257→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
258→    // at the X character on the right           X
259→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
260→        .uppercase()
261→    ```
262→
263→Rule id: `function-signature` (`standard` rule set)
264→
265→## If else bracing
266→
267→If at least one branch of an if-else statement or an if-else-if statement is wrapped between curly braces then all branches should be wrapped between braces.
268→
269→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
270→
271→    ```kotlin
272→    fun foo(value: int) {
273→        if (value > 0) {
274→            doSomething()
275→        } else if (value < 0) {
276→            doSomethingElse()
277→        } else {
278→            doSomethingElse2()
279→        }
280→    }
281→    ```
282→
283→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
284→
285→    ```kotlin
286→    fun foo(value: int) {
287→        if (value > 0)
288→            doSomething()
289→        else if (value < 0) {
290→            doSomethingElse()
291→        } else
292→            doSomethingElse2()
293→    }
294→    ```
295→
296→Rule id: `if-else-bracing` (`standard` rule set)
297→
298→!!! Note
299→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
300→
301→## If else wrapping
302→
303→A single line if-statement should be kept simple. It may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches may not be wrapped in a block.
304→
305→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
306→
307→    ```kotlin
308→    fun foobar() {
309→        if (true) foo()
310→        if (true) foo() else bar()
311→    }
312→    ```
313→
314→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
315→
316→    ```kotlin
317→    fun foobar() {
318→        if (true) if (false) foo() else bar()
319→        if (true) bar() else if (false) foo() else bar()
320→        if (true) { foo() } else bar()
321→        if (true) bar() else { if (false) foo() else bar() }
322→    }
323→    ```
324→
325→Rule id: `if-else-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
326→
327→!!! Note
328→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
329→
330→## Naming
331→
332→### Function naming
333→
334→Enforce naming of function. 
335→
336→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
337→
338→    ```kotlin
339→    fun foo() {}
340→    fun fooBar() {}
341→    ```
342→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint Test"
343→
344→    ```kotlin
345→    @Test
346→    fun `Some name`() {}
347→
348→    @Test
349→    fun do_something() {}
350→    ```
351→
352→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
353→
354→    ```kotlin
355→    fun Foo() {}
356→    fun Foo_Bar() {}
357→    fun `Some name`() {}
358→    fun do_something() {}
359→    ```
360→
361→!!! note
362→    Functions in files which import a class from package `org.junit`, `org.testng` or `kotlin.test` are considered to be test functions. Functions in such classes are allowed to have underscores in the name. Or function names can be specified between backticks and do not need to adhere to the normal naming convention.
363→
364→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `FunctionName`.
365→
366→Rule id: `function-naming` (`standard` rule set)
367→
368→### Package naming
369→
370→Enforce naming of package.
371→
372→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PackageName`.
373→
374→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
375→
376→    ```kotlin
377→    package foo
378→    package foo.foo
379→    package foo_bar
380→    package foo.foo_bar
381→    ```
382→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
383→
384→    ```kotlin
385→    package Foo
386→    package foo.Foo
387→    package `foo bar`
388→    package foo.`foo bar`
389→    ```
390→
391→Rule id: `package-naming` (`standard` rule set)
392→
393→### Property naming
394→
395→Enforce naming of property.
396→
397→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
398→
399→    ```kotlin
400→    val FOO_1 = Foo()
401→    val FOO_BAR_1 = "FOO-BAR"
402→
403→    var foo1: Foo = Foo()
404→
405→    class Bar {
406→        const val FOO_2 = "foo"
407→        const val FOO_BAR_2 = "FOO-BAR"
408→
409→        val foo2 = "foo"
410→        val fooBar2 = "foo-bar"
411→
412→        // Backing property
413→        private val _elementList = mutableListOf<Element>()
414→        val elementList: List<Element>
415→            get() = _elementList
416→    }
417→    ```
418→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
419→
420→    ```kotlin
421→    val Foo1 = Foo()
422→    val FooBar1 = "FOO-BAR"
423→
424→    var FOO_1: Foo = Foo()
425→
426→    class Bar {
427→        const val foo2 = "foo"
428→        const val fooBar2 = "FOO-BAR"
429→
430→        val FOO2 = "foo"
431→        val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar"
432→
433→        // Incomplete backing property as public property 'elementList1' is missing
434→        private val _elementList1 = mutableListOf<Element>()
435→
436→        // Invalid backing property as '_elementList2' is not a private property
437→        val _elementList2 = mutableListOf<Element>()
438→        val elementList2: List<Element>
439→            get() = _elementList2
440→    }
441→    ```
442→
443→!!! note
444→    Top level `val` properties and `const val` properties have to be written in screaming snake notation. Local `val` and `const val` are written in lower camel case.
445→
446→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PropertyName`.
447→
448→Rule id: `property-naming` (`standard` rule set)
449→
450→## No empty file
451→
452→A kotlin (script) file should not be empty. It needs to contain at least one declaration. Files only contain a package and/or import statements are as of that disallowed.
453→
454→Rule id: `no-empty-file`
455→
456→## No single line block comments
457→
458→A single line block comment should be replaced with an EOL comment when possible.
459→
460→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
461→
462→    ```kotlin
463→    /*
464→     * Some comment
465→     */
466→    val foo = "foo" // Some comment
467→    val foo = { /* no-op */ }
468→
469→    /* ktlint-disable foo-rule-id bar-rule-id */
470→    val foo = "foo"
471→    /* ktlint-enable foo-rule-id bar-rule-id */
472→    ```
473→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
474→
475→    ```kotlin
476→    /* Some comment */
477→    val foo = "foo" /* Some comment */
478→    ```
479→
480→Rule id: `no-single-line-block-comment` (`standard` rule set)
481→
482→## Spacing
483→
484→### No blank lines in list
485→
486→Disallow blank lines to be used in lists before the first element, between elements, and after the last element.
487→
488→*Super type*
489→
490→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
491→
492→    ```kotlin
493→    class FooBar:
494→        Foo,
495→        Bar {
496→        // body
497→    }
498→    ```
499→
500→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
501→
502→    ```kotlin
503→    class FooBar:
504→
505→        Foo,
506→
507→        Bar
508→
509→    {
510→        // body
511→    }
512→    ```
513→
514→*Type argument list*
515→
516→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
517→
518→    ```kotlin
519→    val foobar: FooBar<
520→        Foo,
521→        Bar,
522→        > = FooBar(Foo(), Bar())
523→    ```
524→
525→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
526→
527→    ```kotlin
528→    val foobar: FooBar<
529→
530→        Foo,
531→
532→        Bar,
533→
534→        > = FooBar(Foo(), Bar())
535→    ```
536→
537→*Type constraint list*
538→
539→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
540→
541→    ```kotlin
542→    class BiAdapter<C : RecyclerView.ViewHolder, V1 : C, V2 : C, out A1, out A2>(
543→        val adapter1: A1,
544→        val adapter2: A2
545→    ) : RecyclerView.Adapter<C>()
546→        where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>, A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
547→              A2 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V2>, A2 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider {
548→        // body
549→    }
550→    ```
551→
552→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
553→
554→    ```kotlin
555→    class BiAdapter<C : RecyclerView.ViewHolder, V1 : C, V2 : C, out A1, out A2>(
556→        val adapter1: A1,
557→        val adapter2: A2
558→    ) : RecyclerView.Adapter<C>()
559→        where
560→              A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>, A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
561→
562→              A2 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V2>, A2 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider
563→    {
564→        // body
565→    }
566→    ```
567→
568→*Type parameter list*
569→
570→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
571→
572→    ```kotlin

574→        Foo,
575→        Bar,
576→        > foobar()
577→    ```
578→
579→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
580→
581→    ```kotlin
582→    fun <
583→
584→        Foo,
585→
586→        Bar,
587→
588→        > foobar()
589→    ```
590→
591→*Value argument list*
592→
593→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
594→
595→    ```kotlin

597→        "foo",

599→    )
600→    ```
601→
602→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
603→
604→    ```kotlin

606→
607→        "foo",
608→

629→
630→        foo: String,

632→        bar: String,

634→    )

639→!!! Note
640→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
641→
642→### Parameter list spacing
643→
644→Consistent spacing inside the parameter list.
645→
646→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
647→
648→    ```kotlin
649→    fun foo(a: Any ) = "some-result"
650→    fun foo() = "some-result"
651→    ```
652→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
653→
654→    ```kotlin
655→    fun foo( a : Any ) = "some-result"
656→    fun foo(
657→    ) = "some-result"
658→    ```
659→
660→Rule id: `parameter-list-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
661→
662→### String template indent
663→
664→Enforce consistent string template indentation for multiline string templates which are post-fixed with `.trimIndent()`. The opening and closing `"""` are placed on separate lines and the indentation of the content of the template is aligned with the `"""`.
665→
666→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
667→
668→    ```kotlin
669→    val foo =
670→        """
671→        line1
672→        line2
673→        """.trimIndent()
674→    fun foo() {
675→        // The opening """ can not be wrapped to next line as that would result in a compilation error
676→        return """
677→            line1
678→            line2
679→            """.trimIndent()
680→    }
681→    ```
682→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
683→
684→    ```kotlin
685→    val foo = """
686→              line1
687→              line2
688→              """.trimIndent()
689→    fun foo() {
690→        return """
691→            line1
692→            line2
693→        """.trimIndent()
694→    }
695→    ```
696→
697→Rule id: `string-template-indent` (`standard` rule set)
698→
699→!!! Note
700→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
701→
702→### Try catch finally spacing
703→
704→Enforce consistent spacing in `try { .. } catch { .. } finally { .. }`.
705→
706→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
707→
708→    ```kotlin
709→    fun foo() =
710→        try {
711→            // do something
712→        } catch (exception: Exception) {
713→            // handle exception
714→        } finally {
715→            // clean up
716→        }
717→    ```
718→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
719→
720→    ```kotlin
721→    fun foo1() = try { /* ... */ } catch (exception: Exception) { /* ... */ } finally { /* ... */ }
722→    fun foo2() = 
723→        try {
724→            // do something
725→        }
726→        catch (exception: Exception) {
727→            // handle exception
728→        }
729→        finally {
730→            // clean up
731→        }
732→    ```
733→
734→Rule id: `try-catch-finally-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
735→
736→!!! Note
737→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
738→
739→### Type argument list spacing
740→
741→Spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type argument list.
742→
743→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
744→
745→    ```kotlin
746→    val res = ArrayList<LintError>()
747→    class B<T> : A<T>() {
748→        override fun x() = super<A>.x()
749→    }
750→    ```
751→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
752→
753→    ```kotlin
754→    val res = ArrayList < LintError > ()
755→    class B<T> : A< T >() {
756→        override fun x() = super< A >.x()
757→    }
758→    ```
759→
760→Rule id: `type-argument-list-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
761→
762→### Type parameter list spacing
763→
764→Spacing after a type parameter list in function and class declarations.
765→
766→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
767→
768→    ```kotlin
769→    fun <T> foo1(t: T) = "some-result"
770→    fun <T> foo2(t: T) = "some-result"
771→    fun <T> foo3(t: T) = "some-result"
772→    ```
773→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
774→
775→    ```kotlin
776→    fun<T> foo1(t: T) = "some-result"
777→    fun <T>foo2(t: T) = "some-result"
778→    fun<T>foo3(t: T) = "some-result"
779→    ```
780→
781→Rule id: `type-parameter-list-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
782→
783→## Wrapping
784→
785→### Content receiver wrapping
786→
787→Wraps the content receiver list to a separate line regardless of maximum line length. If the maximum line length is configured and is exceeded, wrap the context receivers and if needed its projection types to separate lines.
788→
789→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
790→
791→    ```kotlin
792→    // ALways wrap regardless of whether max line length is set
793→    context(Foo)
794→    fun fooBar()
795→
796→    // Wrap each context receiver to a separate line when the
797→    // entire context receiver list does not fit on a single line
798→    context(
799→        Fooooooooooooooooooo1,
800→        Foooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo2
801→    )
802→    fun fooBar()
803→
804→    // Wrap each context receiver to a separate line when the
805→    // entire context receiver list does not fit on a single line.
806→    // Also, wrap each of it projection types in case a context
807→    // receiver does not fit on a single line after it has been
808→    // wrapped.
809→    context(
810→        Foooooooooooooooo<
811→            Foo,
812→            Bar
813→            >
814→    )
815→    fun fooBar()
816→    ```
817→
818→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
819→
820→    ```kotlin
821→    // Should be wrapped regardless of whether max line length is set
822→    context(Foo) fun fooBar()
823→
824→    // Should be wrapped when the entire context receiver list does not
825→    // fit on a single line
826→    context(Fooooooooooooooooooo1, Foooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo2)
827→    fun fooBar()
828→
829→    // Should be wrapped when the entire context receiver list does not
830→    // fit on a single line. Also, it should wrap each of it projection
831→    // type in case a context receiver does not fit on a single line 
832→    // after it has been wrapped.
833→    context(Foooooooooooooooo<Foo, Bar>)
834→    fun fooBar()
835→    ```
836→
837→Rule id: `context-receiver-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
838→
839→### Enum wrapping
840→
841→An enum should be a single line, or each enum entry has to be placed on a separate line. In case the enumeration contains enum entries and declarations those are to be separated by a blank line.
842→
843→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
844→
845→    ```kotlin
846→    enum class Foo { A, B, C, D }
847→
848→    enum class Foo {
849→        A,
850→        B,
851→        C,
852→        D,
853→        ;
854→
855→        fun foo() = "foo"
856→    }
857→    ```
858→
859→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
860→
861→    ```kotlin
862→    enum class Foo {
863→        A,
864→        B, C,
865→        D
866→    }
867→
868→    enum class Foo {
869→        A;
870→        fun foo() = "foo"
871→    }
872→    ```
873→
874→Rule id: `enum-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
875→
876→### Multiline expression wrapping
877→
878→Multiline expression on the right hand side of an expression are forced to start on a separate line. Expressions in return statement are excluded as that would result in a compilation error. 
879→
880→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
881→
882→    ```kotlin
883→    val foo =
884→        foo(
885→            parameterName =
886→                "The quick brown fox "
887→                    .plus("jumps ")
888→                    .plus("over the lazy dog"),
889→        )
890→    ```
891→
892→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
893→
894→    ```kotlin
895→    val foo = foo(
896→        parameterName = "The quick brown fox "
897→            .plus("jumps ")
898→            .plus("over the lazy dog"),
899→    )
900→    ```
901→
902→Rule id: `multiline-expression-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
903→
904→!!! Note
905→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
906→
907→### Statement wrapping
908→
909→A function, class/object body or other block body statement has to be placed on different line than the braces of the body block.
910→
911→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
912→
913→    ```kotlin
914→    fun foo() {
915→        if (true) {
916→            // do something
917→        }
918→    }
919→    class A {
920→        val a = 0
921→        val b = 1
922→    }
923→    ```
924→
925→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
926→
927→    ```kotlin
928→    fun foo() { if (true) {
929→            // do something
930→        }
931→    }
932→    class A { val a = 0
933→        val b = 1 }
934→    ```
935→
936→Rule id: `statement-wrapping`

 40→/**
 41→ * Execute [KtLintRuleEngine.lint] on given code snippet. To test a kotlin script file, provide a filepath ending with
 42→ * ".kts". For each invocation of this method, a fresh instance of the [KtLintRuleEngine] is instantiated for the given
 43→ * set of rules.
 44→ * This method is intended to be executed in a unit test environment only. If the project that is containing the unit
 45→ * test contains an '.editorconfig' file, then it will be ignored entirely. Provide '.editorconfig' properties that have
 46→ * to be applied on the code snippet via [editorConfigOverride].
 47→ */
 48→@Deprecated("Marked for removal in Ktlint 0.50. Raise an issue if you actually are using this function.")
 49→public fun Set<RuleProvider>.lint(
 50→    text: String,
 51→    filePath: String? = null,
 52→    editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 53→): List<LintError> {
 54→    val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
 55→    val code =
 56→        if (filePath != null) {
 57→            Code.fromFile(KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath).toFile())
 58→        } else {
 59→            Code.fromSnippet(text)
 60→        }
 61→    KtLintRuleEngine(
 62→        ruleProviders = this,
 63→        editorConfigOverride =
 64→            editorConfigOverride
 65→                .enableExperimentalRules()
 66→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(this),
 67→        fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
 68→    ).lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
 69→    return lintErrors
 70→}
 71→
 72→/**
 73→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
 74→ * provided.
 75→ */
 76→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
 77→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =
 78→        ruleProviders
 79→            .asSequence()
 80→            .map { ruleProvider ->
 81→                ruleProvider
 82→                    .createNewRuleInstance()
 83→                    .ruleId
 84→                    .ruleSetId
 85→                    .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
 86→            }.distinct()
 87→            .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
 88→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
 89→            .toList()
 90→            .toTypedArray()
 91→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
 92→}
 93→
 94→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
 95→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
 96→
 97→/**
 98→ * Execute [KtLintRuleEngine.format] on given code snippet. To test a kotlin script file, provide a filepath ending with
 99→ * ".kts". For each invocation of this method, a fresh instance of the [KtLintRuleEngine] is instantiated for the given
100→ * set of rules.
101→ * This method is intended to be executed in a unit test environment only. If the project that is containing the unit
102→ * test contains an '.editorconfig' file, then it will be ignored entirely. Provide '.editorconfig' properties that have
103→ * to be applied on the code snippet via [editorConfigOverride].
104→ */
105→@Deprecated("Marked for removal in Ktlint 0.50. Raise an issue if you actually are using this function.")
106→public fun Set<RuleProvider>.format(
107→    text: String,
108→    filePath: String?,
109→    editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
110→): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
111→    val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
112→    val code =
113→        if (filePath != null) {
114→            Code.fromFile(KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath).toFile())
115→        } else {
116→            Code.fromSnippet(text)
117→        }
118→    val formattedCode =
119→        KtLintRuleEngine(
120→            ruleProviders = this,
121→            editorConfigOverride =
122→                editorConfigOverride
123→                    .enableExperimentalRules()
124→                    .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(this),
125→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
126→        ).format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
127→    return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
128→}

132→// not be allowed to write any content on the file system.
133→// As this FileSystem is not modified it is not considered to be a problem that the file system is not closed after each unit test.
134→private val KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM = KtlintTestFileSystem()

 51→/**
 52→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 53→ *
 54→ * ```
 55→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 56→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 57→ *
 58→ * @Test
 59→ * fun `Some test`() {
 60→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 61→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 62→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 63→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 64→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 65→ * }
 66→ * ```
 67→ */
 68→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 69→public class KtLintAssertThat(
 70→    /**
 71→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 72→     */
 73→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
 74→    /**
 75→     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 76→     */
 77→    private val code: String,
 78→    /**
 79→     * Providers of rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note
 80→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 81→     */
 82→    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
 83→    /**
 84→     * EditorConfig properties to be applied by default when linting/formatting the code.
 85→     */
 86→    private val editorConfigProperties: MutableSet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>,
 87→) {
 88→    private var filePath: String? = null
 89→    private var kotlinScript = false
 90→
 91→    /**
 92→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
 93→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 94→     */
 95→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97→
 98→        return this
 99→    }
100→
101→    /**
102→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
103→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
104→     * silently overwritten.
105→     *
106→     * Example of usage:
107→     * ```
108→     *  val code =
109→     *      """
110→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
111→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
112→     *      """.trimIndent()
113→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
114→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
115→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
116→     * ```
117→     */
118→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
119→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
120→        code
121→            .split("\n")
122→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
123→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
124→            ?.let { index -> editorConfigProperties.add(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString()) }
125→            ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
126→
127→        return this
128→    }
129→
130→    /**
131→     * Handle the code as if it was specified in file on the given path. Only to be used when the [filePath] is absolutely needed. It
132→     * results in creating a file cause a slight performance decrease of the test.
133→     */
134→    public fun asFileWithPath(filePath: String): KtLintAssertThat {
135→        this.filePath = filePath
136→        return this
137→    }
138→
139→    /**
140→     * Handle the code as Kotlin script (kts).
141→     */
142→    public fun asKotlinScript(kotlinScript: Boolean = true): KtLintAssertThat {
143→        this.kotlinScript = kotlinScript
144→        return this
145→    }

147→    /**
148→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used

150→     * indenting.

152→     * Caution:
153→     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
154→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
155→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
156→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
157→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
158→     *
159→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProviders] when adding multiple providers of rules.
160→     */
161→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProvider(provider: () -> Rule): KtLintAssertThat {
162→        additionalRuleProviders.add(RuleProvider(provider))

165→    }

167→    /**
168→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used

326→
327→        /**
328→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
329→         */
330→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
331→
332→        /**
333→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
334→         */
335→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
336→
337→        init {
338→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
339→             * val code =
340→             *     """
341→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
342→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
343→             *     """.trimIndent()
344→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
345→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
346→             */
347→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
348→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
349→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
350→            }
351→        }
352→    }
353→}
354→
355→/**
356→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
357→ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
358→ * assertions operate on the same state.
359→ */
360→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
361→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
362→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
363→    private val code: Code,
364→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
365→    /**
366→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
367→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
368→     */
369→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
370→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
371→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
372→
373→    /**
374→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
375→     * KtLintAssertThat.
376→     *
377→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
378→     */
379→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
380→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
381→
382→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
383→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
384→
385→        assertAll(
386→            {
387→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
388→                    .describedAs(
389→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
390→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
391→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
392→                            } else {
393→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
394→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
395→                            },
396→                    )
397→                    .isEmpty()
398→            },
399→            {
400→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
401→                    .describedAs(
402→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
403→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
404→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
405→                            } else {
406→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
407→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
408→                            },
409→                    )
410→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
411→            },
412→        )
413→    }
414→
415→    /**
416→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
417→     */
418→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
419→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
420→
421→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
422→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '${ruleId.value}' while none were expected")
423→            .isEmpty()
424→
425→        return this
426→    }
427→
428→    /**
429→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
430→     *
431→     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly
432→     * formatted.
433→     *
434→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample
435→     * code, but the code is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each
436→     * other, a unit test cal still verify that code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not
437→     * violated.
438→     */
439→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
440→        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
441→            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
442→        }
443→
444→        val lintErrors = lint()
445→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
446→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
447→
448→        return this
449→    }
450→
451→    /**
452→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar coated version of
453→     * [hasLintViolation] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
454→     */
455→    public fun hasLintViolation(
456→        line: Int,
457→        col: Int,
458→        detail: String,
459→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
460→        hasLintViolations(
461→            LintViolation(
462→                line = line,
463→                col = col,
464→                detail = detail,
465→            ),
466→        )
467→
468→    /**
469→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar coated
470→     * version of [hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
471→     */
472→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
473→        line: Int,
474→        col: Int,
475→        detail: String,
476→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
477→        hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(
478→            LintViolation(
479→                line = line,
480→                col = col,
481→                detail = detail,
482→            ),
483→        )
484→
485→    /**
486→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected.
487→     */
488→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
489→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
490→
491→        val actualLintViolationFields =
492→            lint()
493→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
494→                .toLintViolationsFields()
495→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
496→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
497→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields())
498→        return this
499→    }
500→
501→    /**
502→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by additional rules and which can be
503→     * automatically corrected.
504→     */
505→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
506→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
507→
508→        val actualLintViolationFields =
509→            lint()
510→                .filterAdditionalRulesOnly()
511→                .toLintViolationsFields()
512→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
513→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
514→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields())
515→        return this
516→    }
517→
518→    /**
519→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
520→     */
521→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
522→        check(formattedCode != code.content) {
523→            "Use '.hasNoLintViolations()' instead of '.isFormattedAs(<original code>)'"
524→        }
525→
526→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
527→        val (actualFormattedCode, _) = format()
528→
529→        assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
530→            .describedAs("Code is formatted as")
531→            .isEqualTo(formattedCode)
532→
533→        return this
534→    }
535→
536→    /**
537→     * Asserts that the code contains the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
538→     */
539→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
540→        line: Int,
541→        col: Int,
542→        detail: String,
543→    ): Unit =
544→        hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(
545→            LintViolation(
546→                line = line,
547→                col = col,
548→                detail = detail,
549→            ),
550→        )
551→
552→    /**
553→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically
554→     * corrected.
555→     */
556→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {
557→        check(expectedLintViolations.isNotEmpty())
558→
559→        val actualLintViolationFields =
560→            lint()
561→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
562→                .map {
563→                    LintViolationFields(
564→                        line = it.line,
565→                        col = it.col,
566→                        detail = it.detail,
567→                        canBeAutoCorrected = false,
568→                    )
569→                }.toTypedArray()
570→        val expectedLintViolationFields =
571→            expectedLintViolations
572→                .map {
573→                    LintViolationFields(
574→                        line = it.line,
575→                        col = it.col,
576→                        detail = it.detail,
577→                        canBeAutoCorrected = false,
578→                    )
579→                }
580→
581→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
582→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can not be automatically corrected")
583→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedLintViolationFields.toTypedArray())
584→    }
585→
586→    private fun Array<out LintViolation>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> {
587→        return map {
588→            LintViolationFields(
589→                line = it.line,
590→                col = it.col,
591→                detail = it.detail,
592→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
593→            )
594→        }.toTypedArray()
595→    }
596→
597→    private fun List<LintError>.filterAdditionalRulesOnly() = filter { it.ruleId != ruleId }
598→
599→    private fun List<LintError>.filterCurrentRuleOnly() = filter { it.ruleId == ruleId }
600→
601→    private fun List<LintError>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> =
602→        map {
603→            LintViolationFields(
604→                line = it.line,
605→                col = it.col,
606→                detail = it.detail,
607→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
608→            )
609→        }.toTypedArray()
610→
611→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
612→        val ruleProviders =
613→            setOf(ruleProvider)
614→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
615→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
616→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
617→        val editorConfigOverride =
618→            editorConfigOverride
619→                .enableExperimentalRules()
620→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
621→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
622→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
623→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
624→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
625→        )
626→    }
627→
628→    private fun lint(): List<LintError> {
629→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
630→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
631→        return lintErrors
632→    }
633→
634→    private fun format(): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
635→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
636→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
637→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
638→    }
639→
640→    /* Representation of the field of the [LintError] that should be identical. Note that no comparison can be made
641→     * against the original [LintError] as the [canBeAutoCorrected] flag is excluded from the hashcode.
642→     */
643→    private data class LintViolationFields(
644→        val line: Int,
645→        val col: Int,
646→        val detail: String,
647→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
648→    )
649→}
650→
651→internal class MissingEolMarker : RuntimeException(
652→    """
653→    The first line of the provide code sample should contain text '$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' which is provided by the
654→    constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
655→    '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
656→    """.trimIndent(),
657→)
658→
659→/**
660→ * Expectation of the [LintError]. Contrary to the [LintError] it does not contain the ruleId. The ruleId will be
661→ * derived from the rule for which the AssertThat was created.
662→ */
663→public data class LintViolation(
664→    val line: Int,
665→    val col: Int,
666→    val detail: String,
667→    val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean = true,
668→)
669→
670→/**
671→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
672→ * provided.
673→ */
674→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
675→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =
676→        ruleProviders
677→            .asSequence()
678→            .map { ruleProvider ->
679→                ruleProvider
680→                    .createNewRuleInstance()
681→                    .ruleId
682→                    .ruleSetId
683→                    .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
684→            }.distinct()
685→            .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
686→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
687→            .toList()
688→            .toTypedArray()
689→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
690→}
691→
692→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
693→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
694→

 34→class KtLintTest {
 35→    /**
 36→     * API Consumers directly use the ktlint-core module. Tests in this module should guarantee that the API is kept
 37→     * stable.
 38→     */
 39→    @Nested
 40→    inner class `Given an API consumer` {
 41→        @Nested
 42→        inner class `Given that lint is invoked via the KtLintRuleEngine` {
 43→            @Test
 44→            fun `Given a non empty rule providers and empty userData then do not throw an error`() {
 45→                var numberOfRootNodesVisited = 0
 46→                KtLintRuleEngine(
 47→                    ruleProviders =
 48→                        setOf(
 49→                            RuleProvider {
 50→                                DummyRule { node ->
 51→                                    if (node.isRoot()) {
 52→                                        numberOfRootNodesVisited++
 53→                                    }
 54→                                }
 55→                            },
 56→                        ),
 57→                ).lint(Code.fromSnippet("fun main() {}"))
 58→                assertThat(numberOfRootNodesVisited).isEqualTo(1)
 59→            }
 60→
 61→            @Test
 62→            fun `Given a rule returning errors which can and can not be autocorrected than that state of the error can be retrieved in the callback`() {
 63→                val code =
 64→                    """
 65→                    val foo = "$STRING_VALUE_NOT_TO_BE_CORRECTED"
 66→                    val bar = "$STRING_VALUE_TO_BE_AUTOCORRECTED"
 67→                    """.trimIndent()
 68→                val callbacks = mutableListOf<CallbackResult>()
 69→                KtLintRuleEngine(
 70→                    ruleProviders =
 71→                        setOf(
 72→                            RuleProvider { AutoCorrectErrorRule() },
 73→                        ),
 74→                ).lint(Code.fromSnippet(code)) { e ->
 75→                    callbacks.add(

 83→                        ),
 84→                    )
 85→                }

471→/**
472→ * A dummy rule for testing. Optionally the rule can be created with a lambda to be executed for each node visited.
473→ */
474→private open class DummyRule(
475→    val block: (node: ASTNode) -> Unit = {},
476→) : Rule(
477→    ruleId = RuleId("test:dummy"),
478→    about = About(),
479→) {
480→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
481→        node: ASTNode,
482→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
483→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
484→    ) {
485→        block(node)
486→    }
487→}
488→
489→/**
490→ * A dummy rule for testing
491→ */
492→private class AutoCorrectErrorRule : Rule(
493→    ruleId = AUTOCORRECT_ERROR_RULE_ID,
494→    about = About(),
495→) {
496→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
497→        node: ASTNode,
498→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
499→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
500→    ) {
501→        if (node.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART) {
502→            when (node.text) {
503→                STRING_VALUE_TO_BE_AUTOCORRECTED -> {
504→                    emit(node.startOffset, ERROR_MESSAGE_CAN_BE_AUTOCORRECTED, true)
505→                    if (autoCorrect) {
506→                        (node as LeafElement).rawReplaceWithText(STRING_VALUE_AFTER_AUTOCORRECT)
507→                    }
508→                }
509→                STRING_VALUE_NOT_TO_BE_CORRECTED ->
510→                    emit(node.startOffset, ERROR_MESSAGE_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED, false)
511→            }
512→        }
513→    }
514→
515→    companion object {
516→        val AUTOCORRECT_ERROR_RULE_ID = RuleId("test:auto-correct")
517→        const val STRING_VALUE_TO_BE_AUTOCORRECTED = "string-value-to-be-autocorrected"
518→        const val STRING_VALUE_NOT_TO_BE_CORRECTED = "string-value-not-to-be-corrected"
519→        const val STRING_VALUE_AFTER_AUTOCORRECT = "string-value-after-autocorrect"
520→        const val ERROR_MESSAGE_CAN_BE_AUTOCORRECTED = "This string value is not allowed and can be autocorrected"
521→        const val ERROR_MESSAGE_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED =
522→            "This string value is not allowed but can not be autocorrected"
523→    }
524→}
525→
526→/**
527→ * Rule in style starting from ktlint 0.47.x in which a rule can can override method [Rule.beforeFirstNode],
528→ * [Rule.beforeVisitChildNodes], [Rule.afterVisitChildNodes] and [Rule.afterLastNode]. For each invocation to
529→ * this method a [RuleExecutionCall] is added to the list of previously calls made.
530→ */
531→private class SimpleTestRule(
532→    private val ruleExecutionCalls: MutableList<RuleExecutionCall>,
533→    ruleId: RuleId,
534→    visitorModifiers: Set<VisitorModifier> = emptySet(),
535→    private val stopTraversalInBeforeFirstNode: Boolean = false,
536→    private val stopTraversalInBeforeVisitChildNodes: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { false },
537→    private val stopTraversalInAfterVisitChildNodes: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { false },
538→    private val stopTraversalInAfterLastNode: Boolean = false,
539→) : Rule(
540→    ruleId = ruleId,
541→    about = About(),
542→    visitorModifiers,
543→) {
544→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
545→        ruleExecutionCalls.add(RuleExecutionCall(ruleId, BEFORE_FIRST))
546→        if (stopTraversalInBeforeFirstNode) {
547→            stopTraversalOfAST()
548→        }
549→    }
550→
551→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
552→        node: ASTNode,
553→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
554→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
555→    ) {
556→        ruleExecutionCalls.add(node.toRuleExecutionCall(ruleId, BEFORE_CHILDREN))
557→        if (stopTraversalInBeforeVisitChildNodes(node)) {
558→            stopTraversalOfAST()
559→        }
560→    }
561→
562→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
563→        node: ASTNode,
564→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
565→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
566→    ) {
567→        ruleExecutionCalls.add(node.toRuleExecutionCall(ruleId, AFTER_CHILDREN))
568→        if (stopTraversalInAfterVisitChildNodes(node)) {
569→            stopTraversalOfAST()
570→        }
571→    }
572→
573→    override fun afterLastNode() {
574→        ruleExecutionCalls.add(RuleExecutionCall(ruleId, AFTER_LAST))
575→        if (stopTraversalInAfterLastNode) {
576→            stopTraversalOfAST()
577→        }
578→    }
579→
580→    private fun ASTNode.toRuleExecutionCall(
581→        ruleId: RuleId,
582→        ruleMethod: RuleExecutionCall.RuleMethod,
583→    ) = RuleExecutionCall(
584→        ruleId,
585→        ruleMethod,
586→        visitNodeType,
587→        elementType,
588→        if (elementType == CLASS) {
589→            findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)?.text
590→        } else {
591→            null
592→        },
593→    )
594→
595→    companion object {
596→        val RULE_ID_A = RuleId("simple-test:a")
597→        val RULE_ID_B = RuleId("simple-test:b")
598→        val RULE_ID_C = RuleId("simple-test:c")
599→        val RULE_ID_D = RuleId("simple-test:d")
600→        val RULE_ID_STOP_TRAVERSAL = RuleId("simple-test:stop-traversal")
601→    }
602→}
603→
604→private data class RuleExecutionCall(
605→    val ruleId: RuleId,
606→    val ruleMethod: RuleMethod,
607→    val visitNodeType: VisitNodeType? = null,
608→    val elementType: IElementType? = null,
609→    val classIdentifier: String? = null,
610→) {
611→    enum class RuleMethod { BEFORE_FIRST, BEFORE_CHILDREN, VISIT, AFTER_CHILDREN, AFTER_LAST }
612→
613→    enum class VisitNodeType { ROOT, CHILD }
614→}
615→
616→private val ASTNode.visitNodeType: RuleExecutionCall.VisitNodeType
617→    get() =
618→        if (isRoot()) {
619→            ROOT
620→        } else {
621→            CHILD
622→        }
623→
624→private fun getResourceAsText(path: String) =
625→    (ClassLoader.getSystemClassLoader().getResourceAsStream(path) ?: throw RuntimeException("$path not found"))
626→        .bufferedReader()
627→        .readText()
628→
629→private data class CallbackResult(
630→    val line: Int,
631→    val col: Int,
632→    val ruleId: RuleId,
633→    val detail: String,
634→    val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
635→    val corrected: Boolean,
636→)
637→
638→/**
639→ * This rule throws an exception when it is visited more than once.
640→ */
641→private class WithStateRule : Rule(
642→    ruleId = RuleId("test:with-state"),
643→    about = About(),
644→) {
645→    private var hasNotBeenVisitedYet = true
646→
647→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
648→        check(hasNotBeenVisitedYet) {
649→            "Rule has been visited before"
650→        }
651→        hasNotBeenVisitedYet = false
652→    }
653→
654→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
655→        node: ASTNode,
656→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
657→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
658→    ) {
659→        emit(node.startOffset, "Fake violation which can be autocorrected", true)
660→    }
661→}

190→                            val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
191→                            errors.add(
192→                                Pair(
193→                                    LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
194→                                    // It is assumed that a rule only corrects an error after it has emitted an
195→                                    // error and indicating that it actually can be autocorrected.
196→                                    false,
197→                                ),
198→                            )
199→                        }
200→                    }
201→            }
202→
203→            val formattedCode =
204→                if (!foundAutoCorrectableViolations) {
205→                    codeToFormat.content
206→                } else {
207→                    val formatted =
208→                        ruleExecutionContext
209→                            .rootNode
210→                            .text
211→                            .replace("\n", ruleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(codeToFormat.content))
212→                    if (hasUTF8BOM) {
213→                        UTF8_BOM + formatted
214→                    } else {
215→                        formatted
216→                    }
217→                }
218→
219→            return FormatResult(
220→                formattedCode = formattedCode,
221→                errors = errors.sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col },
222→                foundAutoCorrectableViolations = foundAutoCorrectableViolations,
223→            )
224→        }
225→
226→        // Repeat format until no more violations are found that can be autocorrected.
227→        // To prevent infinite reruns, execute at most 3 reruns.
228→        var codeToFormat = code
229→        var lastResult: FormatResult
230→        var reruns = 0
231→        do {
232→            lastResult = formatOnce(codeToFormat)
233→            if (!lastResult.foundAutoCorrectableViolations || reruns == 3) {
234→                break
235→            }
236→            reruns++
237→            codeToFormat = codeToFormat.copy(content = lastResult.formattedCode)
238→        } while (true)
239→
240→        lastResult
241→            .errors
242→            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
243→
244→        LOGGER.debug("Finished with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'")
245→        return lastResult.formattedCode
246→    }

248→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
249→        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
250→        return when {
251→            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
252→            else -> "\n"
253→        }
254→    }
